title: Latest News from Science Magazine
description: Keyword search result for Alerts: News Weekly Headlines -- New results matching your keyword search query (updated daily).

items.title: Supporters push to revive moribund agency studying patient care
items.description: The Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality has grant money, but nobody to hand it out

items.title: With new environmental law, China expands its ‘war on pollution’
items.description: But observers fear measure will limit public’s ability to challenge potentially harmful projects

items.title: As U.S. blockade bites, Cuba’s health care and science suffer
items.description: Hospitals and researchers struggle to cope with lengthy blackouts and supply shortages

items.title: These small African antelopes may help mpox spread
items.description: Researchers find evidence that duikers, hunted and eaten across sub-Saharan Africa, can harbor the deadly virus

items.title: Two years after it emerged, ‘cow flu’ is still circulating—and baffling scientists
items.description: Researchers still aren’t sure how H5N1 influenza spreads between cows and from farm to farm

items.title: ‘Milestone’ research method measures gene activity across whole mice
items.description: New way to analyze frozen tissue slices could reveal bodywide effects of drugs, diseases

items.title: These birds suck—literally
items.description: Scientists spot first example of vertebrate drinking with suction from the tongue alone

items.title: Jupiter’s weather forecast: cloudy with a chance of nukes
items.description: The planet’s lightning storms can unleash the force of multiple nuclear weapons every minute

items.title: Abnormal behaviors in lab monkeys may reflect a lifetime of stressful experiences
items.description: Pacing and hair pulling may develop after multiple experiments, years of relative isolation, and more

items.title: ‘Resurrection plants’ bounce back after years of drought. Do they hold lessons for crops?
items.description: Plant biologist Jill Farrant hopes “desiccation-tolerant” species can teach her how to make crops more resilient
